situation.reason_for_admission: Transferred from ED after hypotension, hypoxia, and elevated lactate
situation.current_status: Hemodynamically unstable, on vasopressor support with high-flow oxygen

background.relevant_history: Hypertension, Type 2 diabetes mellitus, CKD stage 2

recommendation.care_plan: Continue sepsis bundle, titrate norepinephrine to maintain MAP >= 65 mmHg, continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending cultures
recommendation.escalation_triggers: MAP < 65, SpO2 < 88%, altered mental status, urine output < 30 mL/hr
pending_orders: Pharmacy antibiotic review for allergy safety
recommendation.next_steps: Q1h vitals, strict I/O, family update at 0800, reassess pressor need after repeat labs

risk_alert_candidates.description: Piperacillin-Tazobactam includes penicillin in a patient with documented penicillin anaphylaxis
SpO2 91% on high-flow oxygen indicates persistent hypoxemia
BP 88/54 suggests ongoing shock despite vasopressor therapy

transcript_seed: Patient Sarah Mitchell in ICU 4B, 67-year-old admitted with septic shock from pneumonia. On norepinephrine, low blood pressure 88/54, heart rate 118, O2 sat 91% on high-flow, temp 38.9. Allergic to penicillin. Awaiting blood cultures and repeat lactate. Escalate for MAP below 65 or saturation below 88.
